title: Support `code = $fread( mem, fd, , count);`
body:
The standard in section _21.3.4.4 Reading binary data_ specifies the syntax `code = $fread( mem, fd, , count);`. Verilator does not recognize it.

```
./run-verilator.sh 
%Error: riscv_gdb_stub_tb.sv:86:33: syntax error, unexpected ',', expecting IDENTIFIER-for-type
   86 |       code = $fread(buffer, fd, , 1);
      |                                 ^
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.037 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
./run-verilator.sh: line 6: obj_dir/Vriscv_gdb_stub_tb: No such file or directory
```

I can probably write a regression testcase for this one and provide it as a pull request.
code_blocks:
./run-verilator.sh

   86 |       code = $fread(buffer, fd, , 1);
      |                                 ^
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.037 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
./run-verilator.sh: line 6: obj_dir/Vriscv_gdb_stub_tb: No such file or directory
